name: Jahan Dotson
headline: Dotson lands in Atlanta hunting a real target share
body:
Two years buried behind A.J. Brown and DeVonta Smith left almost nothing on Dotson's profile. In 2025 he ran 376 routes for just 36 targets, a 7.8% share, and 18 catches for 262 yards and a single score. The efficiency was brutal: 0.7 yards per route run, a 33.3% success rate, and minus-0.418 EPA per play, ranking him 104th. He stayed on the field for 15 games but the role never materialized.

The upside lives in 2023, when Washington gave him 83 targets, a 13.8% share, and 49 grabs for 518 yards and four touchdowns. A two-year Atlanta deal reopens that door, but he is one of several bodies brought in to replace Darnell Mooney inside a run-leaning offense. Until he separates from that committee, treat him as a late-round dart and bench depth, not a weekly starter. ADP near WR125 reflects exactly that.
